extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won a wet flat stage sprint after a late crash eliminated Gaviria, beating Milan in photo finish with minimal team support
tactical_patterns: Ackermann won with only Gibbons as leadout support, suggesting elite sprinters can still win without full trains in reduced pelotons.
Caja Rural attempted to thin the sprint field for their sprinter by forcing pace, indicating teams exploit reduced rosters tactically.
Milan extended points jersey lead at intermediate sprint 78km out, showing deliberate focus on classification beyond stage wins.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: first Giro stage win since 2019 with minimal team support reveals he's regained top sprint form
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: fought for photo finish and actively contested intermediate sprint showing dual focus on stages and points classification
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: suffering with illness again suggests ongoing form/health issues compromising sprint competitiveness
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: crashed out on routine wet descent ending GC ambitions, was third overall before stage
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: looked brilliant in sprint but third place suggests age/timing limitations remain despite strong form
